extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ attacked from start in Stage 20 Monte Lussari mountain TT, overcame 26-second deficit to win Giro by 14 seconds over THOMAS who cracked on final climb.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS's bike change included helmet swap costing critical seconds at bottom; gear optimization matters even in brief transitions.
ROGLIČ gained 32 seconds in middle climb section not at steepest ramps, suggesting pacing strategy targeted sustained power zones.
POZZOVIVO suffered mechanical while leading, exposing risk of pushing equipment limits on 22% gradient ramps in mountain TTs.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: 42-second margin over THOMAS in 19km TT shows superior climbing TT form compared to 2020 Planche des Belles Filles collapse
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: lost 40+ seconds on final climb section after looking competitive at base, suggests inability to sustain threshold at altitude after three-week effort
rider_signals.McNULTY Brandon: positive: set early reference time as domestique, confirming UAE's depth in high-altitude climbing TTs
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: held third at 59 seconds deficit, consistent but lacked explosive power to challenge top two in decisive format
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: negative: mechanical failure while leading reveals equipment pushed beyond reliability limits on extreme gradients
